institution: Sector-wide typology - Indian retail digital payments
source_documents: https://www.npci.org.in (UPI circulars - collect-request limits, fraud advisories)
category: Digital payment fraud - malicious UPI deep-link (intent) phishing
risk_signals: A shared link auto-opens a UPI app with payee and amount pre-filled, ready to authorise
Links delivered via SMS/chat/email from unknown senders with an urgent pretext
Payment screen appears without the user having initiated a transfer
analyst_reasoning: UPI 'intent' deep links (upi://) can pre-fill a payee and amount and launch the payment app directly. A victim who taps through and enters the PIN pays the fraudster. The manipulation is that the payment appears unprompted, exploiting habitual PIN entry.
investigation_steps: Recover the intent link and decode the embedded payee VPA and amount
Identify the delivery channel and the phishing pretext
Check whether the payee VPA appears across multiple victim reports
decision: Block the payee VPA, warn against tapping payment links from unknown sources, and report the phishing infrastructure.
outcome: If the PIN is entered the debit completes; unentered links cause no loss, so awareness stops most attempts.
lessons_learned: A payment screen you did not initiate is a red flag - never enter a PIN on an unprompted request
Deep links from unknown senders should never be tapped
Only the PIN step completes a debit, so pausing there prevents the loss
